raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":1.0,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":1.0,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"Improving the draft centrally involves refining the thesis, contribution claim, and validation-free framing above the sentence level.","R1.2":"The artifact contains an apparent contradiction between buffer-free operation and a proof relying on a validation buffer, so logical consistency is load-bearing.","R1.3":"The empirical cost and quality claims are used to justify the method's strength, so improvement should check whether the stated evidence supports those claims.","R1.5":"Improving a research draft reasonably includes revisiting the section-level argumentative arc from motivation to theory to experiments.","R1.6":"The delegation explicitly asks to improve the draft, making sentence- and paragraph-level polish a load-bearing responsibility.","RX.1":"Any projected improvement responsibility must preserve uncertainty around unstated experimental, theoretical, and prior-work details.","RX.2":"The draft includes strong causal and comparative claims, so avoiding overclaiming is an active responsibility.","RX.3":"The task is limited to improving the draft rather than executing research, rewriting the artifact here, or auditing external literature.","RX.4":"Potential downstream harm includes overstating empirical guarantees or theoretical validity in a research context.","RX.5":"Projected responsibilities should remain traceable to specific statements in the provided artifact."}}

rationale.R1.1: Improving the draft centrally involves refining the thesis, contribution claim, and validation-free framing above the sentence level.
rationale.R1.2: The artifact contains an apparent contradiction between buffer-free operation and a proof relying on a validation buffer, so logical consistency is load-bearing.
rationale.R1.3: The empirical cost and quality claims are used to justify the method's strength, so improvement should check whether the stated evidence supports those claims.
rationale.R1.5: Improving a research draft reasonably includes revisiting the section-level argumentative arc from motivation to theory to experiments.
rationale.R1.6: The delegation explicitly asks to improve the draft, making sentence- and paragraph-level polish a load-bearing responsibility.
rationale.RX.1: Any projected improvement responsibility must preserve uncertainty around unstated experimental, theoretical, and prior-work details.
rationale.RX.2: The draft includes strong causal and comparative claims, so avoiding overclaiming is an active responsibility.
rationale.RX.3: The task is limited to improving the draft rather than executing research, rewriting the artifact here, or auditing external literature.
rationale.RX.4: Potential downstream harm includes overstating empirical guarantees or theoretical validity in a research context.
rationale.RX.5: Projected responsibilities should remain traceable to specific statements in the provided artifact.
